events.action_description: Announced Amador Energy Storage Project (100MW/200MWh BESS) approximately 11 miles southwest of Canton, Van Zandt County, TX
events.legal_mechanism_raw: N/A — project announcement

events.action_description: Presented Amador BESS project to Van Zandt County Commissioners Court at workshop meeting
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Commissioner's Court presentation

events.entity: Ronald Lane, Nancy White, and 18 other Van Zandt County residents
events.action_description: Filed lawsuit (Cause No. 24-00204) seeking damages, temporary and permanent injunction against BT Amador Storage LLC, Maria Amador, Jose Valedelmar Amador, RES Group LLC, Belltown Power Texas Land 2 LLC, and CSC Trust Company of Delaware
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Petition for Damages, Temporary and Permanent Injunction and Jury Demand — 294th Judicial District Court, Van Zandt County

events.entity: Van Zandt County residents (100+ in attendance)
events.action_description: Public meeting held where residents voiced safety concerns including risk of fire and environmental damage from lithium battery explosion
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Public meeting

events.entity: Van Zandt County Fire Marshal (Kevin Palmer)
events.action_description: Issued order to Taaleri Energia to conform to all applicable NFPA standards per Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233 and NFPA 855 (2020 ed). Required: permit application, UL container test results, site survey, hazard mitigation plan, emergency response plan, permanent water source documentation. Compliance deadline: April 7, 2025
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233; NFPA 855 (2020 ed)

events.entity: Van Zandt County District Attorney (Tonda Curry) / 294th District Court Judge Chris Martin
events.action_description: DA presented ex parte petition for Temporary Restraining Order. Judge Martin signed TRO (Cause No. 25-00067) against Taaleri Energia North America LLC, BT Amador Storage LLC, Amador BESS Holdings Inc., Renewable Energy Systems Americas Inc., and Belltown Texas Power 2 LLC. TRO in place for 14 days, prohibiting batteries from entering the county until NFPA compliance achieved
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Temporary Restraining Order — 294th Judicial District Court, Van Zandt County; Texas Health & Safety Code

events.action_description: Court hearing scheduled for July 24, 2025 to determine whether TRO will be extended or batteries allowed in
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Temporary Restraining Order extension proceeding

events.entity: Van Zandt County officials (Fire Marshal Kevin Palmer, DA Tonda Curry, Commissioner Cliff Williams)
events.action_description: Confirmed TRO still in effect. No compliant safety test results received from developer. Groundwork continues but no batteries permitted in county
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Ongoing TRO enforcement

events.entity: Van Zandt County Fire Marshal Kevin Palmer / County Judge Andy Reese
events.action_description: Press release announcing Van Zandt County Fire Marshal's rejection of Amador BESS documents; letter excerpts state that submitted materials do not satisfy NFPA 855 (2020 ed) and Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233 requirements imposed by the March 21, 2025 fire marshal order
events.legal_mechanism_raw: NFPA 855 (2020 ed); Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233

events.action_description: Unanimously adopted temporary moratorium freezing all proposed green energy developments (solar farms, wind installations, BESS) in unincorporated areas. Passed after hours of public testimony
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Health & Safety Code §121.003 (county police powers for health, safety, and welfare)

events.entity: Delta County Attorney (Jay Garrett) / Texas Attorney General (Ken Paxton)
events.action_description: Delta County filed Request for Attorney General Opinion (RQ-0634-KP) asking whether Texas counties have express statutory or constitutional authority to impose moratoria on commercial utility-scale solar, BESS, wind, and data center facilities. Cited as relevant authority test for Van Zandt County green-energy moratorium and similar measures.
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Government Code §402.042 (Attorney General opinion request)

events.action_description: Passed resolution opposing Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Gillespie County, citing safety, economic, and environmental concerns. Also passed resolution requiring documentation for Harper BESS project
events.legal_mechanism_raw: County resolution (Commissioners Court)

events.action_description: As of this date, Peregrine Energy Solutions had not filed any of the required construction documents with Gillespie County. LCRA connection construction continued.
events.legal_mechanism_raw: County documentation requirements (February 24 resolution)

events.entity: Victor Hollender, Bruce Neitzke, Esther Schneider, Sarah Rivas (Harper residents)
events.action_description: Filed lawsuit in 216th Judicial District Court against Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC and B&CWR Inc. (d/b/a Cactus Construction), seeking temporary and permanent injunction to stop construction of 145MW BESS due to fire risk and chemical contamination concerns, proximity to Harper ISD (~1,700 feet)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Petition for Temporary and Permanent Injunction — 216th Judicial District Court, Gillespie County

events.action_description: Filed Petition in Intervention in 216th Judicial District Court, joining the residents' lawsuit as a plaintiff opposing the Rogers Draw BESS. Simultaneously filed Response in Opposition to Rogers Draw's Rule 91a Motion to Dismiss
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Petition in Intervention — 216th Judicial District Court, Gillespie County (Cause No. 25-18363)

events.action_description: Voted to take action on two energy projects. County announced it would file petition of intervention against Peregrine Energy (confirmed the Nov 26 filing). Residents packed meeting to voice concerns. Evidence of new LCRA utility pole construction found at Harper site
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Commissioner's Court meeting

events.action_description: Issued Stop Work Order to Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC for the Rogers Draw BESS project in Harper, Texas
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Stop Work Order

events.action_description: Court issued Order DENYING Defendant Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC's Rule 91a Motion to Dismiss and Request for Attorneys' Fees; Plaintiffs awarded entitlement to attorneys' fees under TRCP 91a.7
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Rules of Civil Procedure 91a (motion to dismiss for cause of action with no basis in law or fact); TRCP 91a.7 (attorney's fee shifting)

events.action_description: Filed countersuit against Gillespie County in 216th Judicial District Court, claiming the county had initially approved the project and reversed course after citizen pressure. Sought dismissal of county's intervention
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Counterclaim — 216th Judicial District Court

events.action_description: Filed Gillespie County's Answer to Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC's Counterclaims in 216th Judicial District Court; argued threatened injury to public safety is "sufficiently likely" given county infrastructure and emergency response limitations and the commencement of construction at Rogers Draw site; argued county's intervention is a lawful exercise of governmental authority
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Rules of Civil Procedure (answer to counterclaims); 216th Judicial District Court Cause No. 25-18363

events.entity: 216th District Court Judge Albert Patillo / All parties
events.action_description: Agreed temporary injunction signed halting all construction activities on the Rogers Draw BESS project at Harper until final judgment. Trial date to be set
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Agreed Temporary Injunction — 216th Judicial District Court

events.entity: Vesper Energy / Ochoa Energy Storage LLC
events.action_description: Applied for Special Use Permit (SUP) to build Ochoa Energy Storage (500MW BESS) on approximately 24-acre property at 27051 Highway Blvd, Katy, TX. Holds two ERCOT queue positions (Ochoa I & II) representing 750MW cumulative capacity
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Special Use Permit application — City of Katy zoning ordinance

events.entity: Vesper Energy (Community Affairs Manager Alex Rohr)
events.action_description: Held "lunch and learn" community session for local residents to provide details on Ochoa project and energy storage industry
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Community outreach session

events.entity: City of Katy Planning and Zoning Committee
events.action_description: Recommended approval of the Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Planning and Zoning Committee recommendation

events.action_description: Unanimously denied Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage after public hearing with multiple residents voicing opposition. Overrode Planning and Zoning Committee recommendation. Motion by Councilmember Dan Smith, seconded by Councilmember Janet Corte. Concerns cited: fire safety, proximity to Katy High School and Katy ISD Outdoor Learning Center, residential areas, Falcon Point Apartments
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Denial of Special Use Permit — City of Katy zoning authority

events.action_description: Citizens expressed continued opposition at city council meeting. Mayor Pro Tem Chris Harris cited proximity to schools and residential areas
events.legal_mechanism_raw: City Council meeting — public comment

events.action_description: Filed appeal and application for declaratory order with Texas Public Utility Commission (PUC Docket No. 58859, SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508). Argued City of Katy's zoning actions exceed regulatory authority under Public Utility Regulatory Act (PURA). Requested PUC declare city ordinances "null and void." Moved for immediate referral to State Office of Administrative Hearings (SOAH)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Appeal and Application for Declaratory Order — Texas PUC; PURA §39.001(c)(d), §39.151

events.action_description: Filed response to Ochoa's PUC appeal, arguing city ordinances do not interfere with PUC or ERCOT jurisdiction and do not conflict with state authority under PURA
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Response to Appeal — Texas PUC Docket No. 58859

events.action_description: Filed with PUC stating direct interest in the outcome of the case, asserting city's denial affects grid authority. PUC decision delayed
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Intervention / Statement of Interest — Texas PUC Docket No. 58859

events.entity: Public Utility Commission of Texas (Commission Advising) / Commissioners Hjaltman & Johnson
events.action_description: Issued Draft Preliminary Order in Docket No. 58859 (Appeal of Ochoa Energy Storage LLC from Actions of the City of Katy and Application for Declaratory Order); Commission referred matter to ALJ at SOAH and reserved authority to identify additional issues under Tex. Gov't Code §2003.049(e)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: PURA §39.001(c)(d), §39.151; Texas Government Code §2003.049(e); PUC Procedural Rules

events.action_description: Procedural schedule set: Ochoa direct testimony April 10, 2026; City direct testimony May 15, 2026; Staff and ERCOT testimony June 1, 2026; Cross-rebuttal July 6, 2026; Hearing on the Merits August 4-5, 2026
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Procedural Schedule — PUC Docket No. 58859 / SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508

events.action_description: Deadline for Ochoa direct testimony filing per adopted procedural schedule (PUC Docket 58859 / SOAH Docket 473-26-07508)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Procedural Schedule — PUC Docket No. 58859 / SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508

events.action_description: Deadline for City of Katy direct testimony filing per adopted procedural schedule (PUC Docket 58859 / SOAH Docket 473-26-07508)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Procedural Schedule — PUC Docket No. 58859 / SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508

events.action_description: Proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System (~1,100 acres, 150MW solar + 150MW BESS) near Willow City in Gillespie County, TX. AMPYR described as an Indonesian energy investment company with no history of completed projects in Texas or the US
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Project proposal / ERCOT interconnection queue

events.action_description: Communicated in writing to AMPYR Energy that the Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance applies to the proposed Marshall Springs project
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Written notice — Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

events.action_description: Sent letter to AMPYR Energy pressing for answers regarding the proposed 1,000-acre BESS and solar facility, citing concerns about Hill Country landscape, drought, wildfire, flash flooding risk, volunteer fire department capacity, and proximity to rural residences
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Congressional inquiry letter

events.action_description: Passed resolution opposing Solar and BESS projects, specifically targeting the proposed Marshall Springs project. Required Marshall Springs Solar Farm and BESS documentation

events.action_description: Sent formal Notice of Applicability of Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance to the proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System Project

events.action_description: Held information meeting with senior representatives of federal, state, county, and city officials. Attendees included aides to U.S. Senator John Cornyn, U.S. Senator Ted Cruz, U.S. Rep. Chip Roy, Texas Senator Pete Flores, and Texas Representative Ellen Troxclair
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Citizen-organized informational meeting with elected officials

events.action_description: Residents packed commissioners court meeting to voice continued concerns with both the Marshall Springs and Rogers Draw projects

events.action_description: Sent follow-up letter to AMPYR Energy renewing pressure, requesting answers on Chinese components and funding sources, environmental risks, and community impact

events.action_description: Filed lawsuit (Case No. 26-18599) against Marshall Springs Storage LLC, Marshall Springs Solar LLC, and Ampyr Energy USA LLC in Gillespie County District Court

events.action_description: Announced commercial operations of Great Kiskadee Storage (100 MW / 200 MWh, 2-hour duration); first operational asset of SA Grid Solutions JV; equipment provided by Powin

events.action_description: Equinor subsidiary East Point Energy began developing the 250 MW Flat Rock Energy Center on 10 acres at Flat Rock Creek Road, Comfort, Kendall County, TX (adjacent to LCRA substation)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Project initiation

events.entity: Comfort residents (~275+ attendees) / Kendall County officials
events.action_description: More than 275 residents crowded into Comfort Middle School to voice opposition to East Point's Flat Rock Energy Center and Key Capture Energy's adjacent Ringtrail Ridge BESS (3-acre site on 8-acre plat); raised concerns about thermal runaway lithium fires, toxic byproducts, contaminated runoff, and limited rural firefighting capacity
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Public meeting / community opposition

events.action_description: Adopted 2024 International Fire Code and implemented restrictive design measures applicable to BESS facilities; effectively raised compliance burden for proposed projects
events.legal_mechanism_raw: International Fire Code 2024 adoption (Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233)

events.entity: Kendall County Commissioners Court / Kerr County Commissioners Court
events.action_description: Kendall County Commissioners Court voted to move toward establishment of a Sub-Regional Planning Commission ("391 Commission") with Kerr County to strengthen joint opposition to BESS development under Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391; approved resolution requesting TCEQ deny all permits for BESS facilities proposed in the county
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391 (Sub-Regional Planning Commission)

events.action_description: Began strengthening Kendall County safety standards for BESS facilities; consulted with nationally-recognized fire/life-safety expert; implemented protective design requirements
events.legal_mechanism_raw: County BESS safety ordinance

events.entity: State Rep. Ellen Troxclair / Texas Legislature
events.action_description: Filed multiple Texas state bills (HB 5569, HB 5572, HB 1378, SB 819) to give counties authority to regulate BESS facilities, require fire safety standards, and impose setback rules — explicitly motivated by Kendall/Comfort situation
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Legislature 89R session BESS bills

events.entity: East Point Energy (Catherine Chapman, Manager of Community Engagement)
events.action_description: Issued letter formally cancelling the Flat Rock Energy Center: "Due to shifting strategic priorities, East Point Energy's development of the Flat Rock Energy Center will no longer be pursued at this time"
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Project cancellation announcement

events.action_description: Publicly declared victory: "East Point is officially calling off their plans for a battery storage facility" — framed cancellation as a major victory after fierce local opposition
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Press statement / political declaration

events.action_description: Engie acquired Platinum BESS project as part of 6GW Texas portfolio purchased from Belltown Power; project sited near Savoy, TX on US Highway 82 (~17 acre site)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Project acquisition / portfolio transfer

events.action_description: Residents voiced concerns about Fannin County energy facility at public meeting; cited noise, sound, air pollution, and inadequate firefighting capacity for 17-acre lithium battery site
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Public meeting / community opposition

events.entity: Texas state leaders / Fannin County residents
events.action_description: Texas state leaders publicly advocated for Fannin County residents opposing the new battery facility; pressed Engie for compliance and additional safety measures
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Legislative advocacy / public pressure

events.entity: Fannin County Fire Marshal Troy Hudson / Fannin County Commissioners Court
events.action_description: Fannin County Judge called for inspection of the BESS facility before it could become operational; walkthrough scheduled with Engie Services US for May 20, 2025 to ensure NFPA / fire code compliance prior to energization
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Fannin County Fire Marshal enforcement / NFPA 855 compliance review (Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233)

events.entity: Engie North America / Platinum Energy Storage LLC
events.action_description: Filed lawsuit against Fannin County in district court alleging county officials "moved the goalposts at every turn" and obstructed the project's right to do business; Platinum BESS separately filed lawsuits against the individual members of Commissioners Court and Fannin County Fire Marshal Troy Hudson; alleged the county-imposed order is "unlawful"
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Developer civil action against county officials; Texas Civil Practice & Remedies Code; Texas tort law

events.action_description: Residents again voiced opposition to BESS plant at public meeting; expressed dismay that Engie was nearing completion of 300MW standalone BESS despite four years of development without their awareness
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Public meeting / community opposition

events.entity: Kerr County Commissioners Court (Commissioners Letz, Paces, et al.)
events.action_description: Rejected Black Mountain Energy Storage's request for property tax abatement on $87 million BESS project at Reservation Rd / IH-10 adjacent to Mountain Home substation; eight speakers spoke against the project at the meeting; Commissioner Letz: "I don't see your application going anywhere"; Commissioner Paces told developer "I guess you've figured out that you are not welcome in Kerr County"
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Tax abatement denial; Texas Tax Code Chapter 312

events.action_description: Signed two formal resolutions opposing BESS in Kerr County: one to be sent to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ, the BESS-permitting agency); one to be sent to State Rep-elect Wes Virdell and State Senator Pete Flores
events.legal_mechanism_raw: County resolution opposing BESS (Texas Local Government Code Chapter 81)

events.action_description: Sent letter to Black Mountain Energy Storage CEO Rhett Bennett pressing for answers regarding the proposed Mountain Home BESS facility
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Congressional inquiry letter

events.action_description: Held workshop on forming a Chapter 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kendall County to formally oppose BESS development across both counties; commissioners unanimously agreed to share the proposed interlocal agreement with Kendall County
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391 (Sub-Regional Planning Commission)

events.entity: Black Mountain Energy Storage II, LLC / ERCOT
events.action_description: ERCOT generation interconnection request entered queue (ERCOT-25INR0210) for 100.8 MW BESS at "7750 LCRA MOUNTAIN HOME SUB" in Kerr County (queue date 02-01-2025)
events.legal_mechanism_raw: ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process

events.action_description: Approved hire of contracted (part-time) fire marshal and adopted a new legally vetted resolution requiring BESS developers to prove compliance with state safety and fire codes before receiving court approval; rescinded earlier May 2025 resolution as a weaker measure
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Resolution requiring full BESS compliance documentation; Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233

events.entity: Kerr County Commissioner Rich Paces (President, Hill Country Energy SRPC)
events.action_description: Told Lower Colorado River Authority Board of Directors that the 391 SRPC had submitted four coordination notices asking LCRA to consult on proposed BESS sites and that LCRA had refused to meet; called LCRA's refusal a failure of "moral obligation to coordinate planning for BESS with our subregional planning commission"
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 391; LCRA Act

events.entity: Black Mountain Energy Storage / Vitis Energy
events.action_description: Black Mountain Energy Storage sold the 200 MW Apache Hill BESS project to woman-owned IPP Vitis Energy
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Project acquisition / commercial transfer

events.legal_mechanism_raw: Project advancement / EPC milestones

events.action_description: Vitis Energy announced financial close of Apache Hill (200 MW / 400 MWh); commercial operations targeted for July 2026

events.entity: Pecan Plantation residents / Hood County Commissioners Court
events.action_description: Residents urged Hood County Commissioners Court to grant the Hood County Fire Marshal "every tool he needs to inspect these" battery storage facilities; cited proximity to Rainbow's Promise childcare (~0.2 mi), Mambrino Elementary (~1.2 mi), and Mambrino Baptist Church (~1 mi); cited reports of large battery fires and inadequate emergency response for the rural area
events.legal_mechanism_raw: Public meeting / fire marshal enforcement demand

van_zandt.outcome_label: delayed_in_litigation — TRO (Cause No. 25-00067) remains in effect; Feb 11, 2026 county green-energy moratorium halts project; Fire Marshal continues to reject Amador's NFPA 855 documents. Most recent public action: Van Zandt County Fire Marshal rejection of Amador BESS submission on 2026-01-16.

gillespie_rogers.outcome_label: delayed_in_litigation — Agreed Temporary Injunction (Jan 14, 2026) halts all construction at the Harper, TX site pending final judgment; Court denied Rogers Draw's Rule 91a Motion to Dismiss on Dec 12, 2025; trial date to be set. Project remains in active litigation as Cause No. 25-18363, 216th Judicial District Court. Most recent public action: Gillespie County's Answer to Rogers Draw Counterclaims filed 2026-01-12.

katy_ochoa.outcome_label: ongoing_dispute — SUP denial stands; case is fully briefed at Texas PUC (Docket 58859) and SOAH (Docket 473-26-07508). PUC issued Draft Preliminary Order Dec 12, 2025; ERCOT intervened. Procedural schedule: Ochoa direct testimony 2026-03-06, Katy direct testimony 2026-04-13, ERCOT/Staff testimony 2026-05-01, Hearing on the Merits 2026-07-15/16. Most recent public action: City of Katy direct testimony deadline 2026-04-13.

gillespie_marshall.outcome_label: delayed_in_litigation — Gillespie County lawsuit (Case No. 26-18599) filed 2026-03-11 against Marshall Springs Storage LLC, Marshall Springs Solar LLC, and AMPYR Energy USA LLC. ERCOT IA (signed 2026-01-28) stands but project is now subject to active county litigation and Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance enforcement; opposition group fightfbgsolar.com publicly active. No new public docket events on Marshall Springs after 2026-03-11 located as of 2026-04-25. Most recent public action: Gillespie County lawsuit filing 2026-03-11.

hill_sun_valley.outcome_label: Outcome: BUILT — Sun Valley Solar (250 MW) commissioned November 2022; Sun Valley Battery Storage (100 MW / 100 MWh) commissioned and announced operational on 2023-12-19. No resident lawsuit, no opposing county resolution, no SUP/zoning denial, no fire marshal enforcement order, no court injunction/TRO, no county-level moratorium identified.

hidalgo_anemoi.outcome_label: Outcome: BUILT — 200 MW / 400 MWh BESS in Hidalgo County reached commercial operations in summer 2024 (May/June 2024). NADBank board document states: "No public opposition to the Project has been detected." No resident lawsuit, no opposing county resolution, no SUP/zoning denial, no fire marshal enforcement order, no court injunction/TRO, no county-level moratorium identified.

hidalgo_kiskadee.outcome_label: Outcome: BUILT — 100 MW / 200 MWh BESS in Hidalgo County reached commercial operations on 2025-02-18. No resident lawsuit, no opposing county resolution, no SUP/zoning denial, no fire marshal enforcement order, no court injunction/TRO, no county-level moratorium identified.

kendall_flat_rock.outcome_label: withdrawn — East Point Energy formally cancelled the 250 MW Flat Rock Energy Center BESS on 2026-01-27 after sustained county-level opposition, the adoption of restrictive Kendall County BESS standards (2024 IFC + protective designs), and Kendall County's joint 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kerr County to oppose BESS development. State Rep. Ellen Troxclair publicly hailed the cancellation as a victory.

fannin_platinum.outcome_label: ongoing_dispute — 300 MW BESS near Savoy is constructed but pre-energization; held up by Fannin County Fire Marshal enforcement actions and county-imposed conditions; Engie/Platinum filed lawsuits against Fannin County, the individual members of the Commissioners Court, and Fire Marshal Troy Hudson alleging the county "moved the goalposts at every turn." Most recent public action: Nov 26, 2025 residents voiced opposition.

kerr_black_mountain.outcome_label: delayed_in_litigation / ongoing_dispute — 100.8 MW BESS proposed for Reservation Rd at IH-10 (Mountain Home substation), Precinct 4, Kerr County. Kerr County Commissioners Court rejected developer's tax abatement request 2024-07-26; Court adopted two formal resolutions opposing BESS 2024-08-26 and forwarded to TCEQ and state legislators; Kerr County formed Hill Country Energy 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kendall County to oppose BESS. Project was "expected to be fully operational by 2027" per developer but is materially impeded.

hood_apache_hill.outcome_label: under_construction — 200 MW / 400 MWh BESS at 2625 Mambrino Highway, Hood County, adjacent to the 2.4 GW Comanche Peak nuclear plant. Vitis took financial close; commercial operations expected July 2026. Disqualifier-level concerns raised by Pecan Plantation residents demanding Hood County Fire Marshal scrutiny: project sits ~0.2 mi from Rainbow's Promise childcare, ~1.2 mi from Mambrino Elementary, ~1 mi from Mambrino Baptist Church. No formal county resolution, lawsuit, or moratorium adopted as of 2026-04-25, but residents formally raised the issue at the Apr 22, 2026 commissioners court.
